text:
2026 IFI Fatherhood Essay Contest  
 
Student’s Name / Nombre del Estudiante -Anahi Melecio 
Father/Father-Figure Name / Nombre del Padre/Figura Paterna 
-Jesus Melecio 
Deadline:  
March 19 
Grade / Grado -5 
School / Escuela -Rachel carson elementary school 
Email Phone / Teléfono  
"What my father or an important father-figure* means to me" "Lo que mi padre o una figura 
paterna importante* significa para mí" 
                        “Ring,ring” the alarm goes off at 4:30 am.The sun is not even out and I could 
hear and feel the cold chilly air coming from outside.I hear his boots stomping outside 
“pum,pum,pum”. He's walking outside to his car and I feel so proud of him for working hard 
everyday.  
     He’s hardworking because everyday he plays volleyball after work. And he tells me how my         
day was and that makes me happy because he works so hard and still plays with me and 
doesn't have a bad attitude.This will help me in my future because I could be successful like 
him. 
           My dad shows up everyday by working in the winter so my mom could buy food for us 
and so we could eat everyday. My perspective has changed because I get to be a better person 
and understand how he feels.One gift I carry from my dad is to be a better and hardworking 
person. 
                   This has changed me because I'm kinder to my brothers because I help them find 
something like their phone and because if they don't want to go to the store I go with them. If  
my dad was not with me I would be mean,and not help anyone or be mean to my friends by 
getting mad at them.One gift I carry from my dad is being hardworking and kind.

Father/Grandfather/Step-Dad/Father-Figure reaction to this essay:  Reacción de 
padre/abuelo/padrastro/figura paterna a este ensayo:  
 Writing About / Escribiendo Sobre:  Father / Padre  
 Grandfather / Abuelo  
 Stepdad / Padrasto  
 Father-Figure / Figura Paterna 3000 Character Maximum / Máximo de Caracteres  
2000 Character Maximum / Máximo de Caracteres   
* Father-Figure can be a stepdad, grandfather, uncle, pastor/rabbi/shaman, God, or an ideal vision of a 
father. *Una figura paterna puede ser un padrastro, un abuelo, un tío, un pastor/rabino/chamán, Dios, o un visión ideal de un 
padre.   
CONTEST DETAILS  
Each student is asked to write a 3000 character (or less) essay on the theme “What My Father 
or an Important Father-Figure Means to Me,” describing their relationship with their father or 
father figure. Students should share their true feelings. Provide examples of joys, frustrations or

special  
memories they have as well as commenting about what they would most like to see in their 
relationship. A father-figure can be a stepdad, grandfather, uncle, coach, pastor/rabbi/shaman,  
God, or an ideal vision of a father.   
CRITERIA FOR ESSAY JUDGING  
Submissions will be evaluated according to grade level. Judging criteria will consist of:  
1. How the essay describes the relationship.  
2. Detail and stories that demonstrate the character of the father/father-figure. 
3. Honesty, clarity, simplicity of writing.  
4. Overall grade level appropriate writing skill.  
JUDGING PROCESS  
1. Essays will be divided into the 13 grade levels (K-12).  
2. Volunteers will use the above judging criteria, reading every essay. Top essays from 
the first round will be sent to a final essay reading session.  
3. At the final essay reading, volunteers will then select 10 essays per grade. Up to 130 
essay semi-finalists will receive special recognition.  
4. The top 13 essay writers will be invited to the IFI Fatherhood Dinner Celebration. 
5. All entrants will receive a Certificate of Participation.  
RULES  
1. One entry per person.  
2. All essays must be original, unaided by AI and created by the student. 3. All 
entries become the property of Illinois Fatherhood Initiative (IFI). IFI will not be able 
to return them.  
4. Entry into the contest transfers copyright of the essay to IFI and grants IFI 
permission to reproduce without compensation all or any portion of the essay and 
response on an unlimited, world-wide basis in its publications and media 
channels.  
5. When appropriate and with parent/guardian permission, IFI will cite the name of the 
author(s) if significant portions of a positive essay/response are used. IFI will also 
change or conceal names to maintain confidentiality if requested.  
6. Contest is open to Illinois students (K-12).  
7. Deadline for upload or postmark is March 19th.  
8. Completed forms can be uploaded on 4dads.org or mailed to:  
1515 S. Grove Avenue, #3667, Barrington, IL 60011.
